figures.caption: Figure 8. Reconstruction results of MoIDM. The semantic-aware IDM performs feature-level reconstruction without visualization.
Figure 9. Visualization results of MoIDM attention heatmaps. The attention consistently localizes on action-critical regions.
Figure 10. Qualitative results on the CALVIN, LIBERO, and LIBERO-Plus benchmarks.
Table 13. Comparing alternative IDM designs on CALVIN ABC-D. We compare the proposed three-IDM decomposition against several single-branch alternatives. The best results are bolded.
Figure 14. Qualitative results of in-distribution real-world evaluation.

Figure 1. Differences among (a) Vision–Language–Action models, (b) Video-Action models, and (c) our MoLA. Our MoLA leverages a mixture of pretrained inverse dynamics models to infer a mixture of latent actions, which bridges video imagination and policy execution, enabling strong performance on both benchmarks and real-world robots.

Table 1. Evaluation results on the CALVIN ABC-D benchmark. MoLA achieves strong performance on all tasks, demonstrating effective generalization. The best results are bolded. * indicates results reproduced by us or reported in previous work.
Figure 13. Training and evaluation settings for our real-world experiments.
Table 6. Dataset statistics in the video generation model pretraining. Number of trajectories used from each dataset during video generation model pretraining.

Figure 12. Sensitivity of MoLA to video generation quality. In one case, the generated future deviates from the task-relevant evolution and causes failure; in the other, the policy still succeeds despite action prediction errors in the generated video.
Figure 4. Data efficiency comparison on CALVIN ABC-D. Performance of pretrained policies fine-tuned using different fractions of the training data.

Figure 2. Overview of MoLA. Left: Pretraining of the mixture of inverse dynamics models (MoIDM). Modality-specific inverse dynamics models (e.g., flow-aware, semantic-aware, and depth-aware IDMs) are independently trained to extract and discretize fine-grained motion information from pairs of current and future frames. Right: End-to-end fine-tuning of the full model. The frozen video generation model is integrated with the pretrained MoIDM and the action head, while MoIDM and the action head are jointly optimized to generate executable action sequences.
Figure 16. Qualitative results of out-of-distribution real-world evaluation.
Figure 11. Qualitative results of failure cases.
Figure 6. Qualitative results of the video generation model on primary and wrist views.

Figure 7. Qualitative analysis of one-step denoising. A single denoising step preserves control-relevant temporal structure and provides MoIDM with reliable transition cues, even without producing a fully photorealistic future video.
Figure 15. Qualitative results of out-of-distribution real-world evaluation.

Figure 3. Experiments are conducted on the CALVIN ABC-D, LIBERO, and LIBERO-Plus benchmarks, as well as on a realworld UR5e robot. We evaluate MoLA across three simulated benchmarks and the real-world setup.
